statement.focus_question: \]Does there exist an infinite Sidon set $A$ such that\[\liminf_{x\to \infty} \frac{\lvert A\cap [1,x]\rvert}{x^{1/2}}(\log x)^c>0\]for some $c>0$?
statement.normalized: Let $A\subset\mathbb{N}$ be an infinite Sidon set. Is it true that\[\liminf_{x\to \infty} \frac{\lvert A\cap [1,x]\rvert}{x^{1/2}}(\log x)^{1/2}=0?\]Does there exist an infinite Sidon set $A$ such that\[\liminf_{x\to \infty} \frac{\lvert A\cap [1,x]\rvert}{x^{1/2}}(\log x)^c>0\]for some $c>0$?
statement.original: Let $A\subset\mathbb{N}$ be an infinite Sidon set. Is it true that\[\liminf_{x\to \infty} \frac{\lvert A\cap [1,x]\rvert}{x^{1/2}}(\log x)^{1/2}=0?\]Does there exist an infinite Sidon set $A$ such that\[\liminf_{x\to \infty} \frac{\lvert A\cap [1,x]\rvert}{x^{1/2}}(\log x)^c>0\]for some $c>0$?
